summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a rare pre‑1970 set, highlighted by first performances of Clementine, Spanish Jam and Born Cross‑Eyed with flawless transitions.
review: The recording is praised as crystal‑clear and fully balanced, letting every instrument—from Pigpen’s keyboards to Jerry’s spacious New Potato guitar—shine through. The show itself is described as a treasure trove of rarities with smooth segues that make it one of the band’s most compelling pre‑Anthem of the Sun performances. Overall fan sentiment is overwhelmingly positive despite a lone complaint about missing track information.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews overwhelmingly praise both the musical performance (rare songs, tight transitions) and the audio quality (excellent soundboard), yielding a high combined score; confidence is medium because detailed technical comments come from only one review.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audio described as excellent, crisp and clear with every instrument audible; smooth mix and no reported drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Transitions called "effortless" and among the smoothest in Dead history; rare jams flow seamlessly from one to another.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for spacious New Potato guitar work and lead vocals on Clementine.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for writing Clementine and his Coltrane influence; noted as a major contributor.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen on keyboards described as being at his best throughout the set.
